rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT GRANT KWOK MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Granted as to the motion to set aside default and to quash substituted service of summons. On July 1, 2013, Plaintiff withdrew his opposition to Mr. Kwok's motion to set aside default and to quash service of summons. Plaintiff now states that Mr. Kwok has been personally served with a summons and complaint. The Court finds that the filing of the Second Amended Complaint alleges additional different facts than the Complaint, and the service of summons runs from the date of amendment. (Barrington v. A. H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, 154.) Now that the default has been set aside, Defendant Kwok may respond to the Second Amended Complaint in compliance with the timing requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Set One. Nos 5 And 7
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs ADRIAN FOX'S, WILLIAM MCMULLEN, JR.'S, and SCOTT WINKLER'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production of Documents, Set One and First Set of Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 5 and 7 Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The parties are instructed to have their respective individuals with database expertise available for the hearing either in-person or via telephone. The designated individual or individuals from Nissan North America (NNA) should be prepared to provide information to the court concerning: (1) the number of results that would be returned from the Consumer Affairs, Techline and Salesforc.com databases by application of the 22 search terms proposed by Plaintiffs that NNA has agreed to versus application of all 25 search terms proposed by Plaintiffs; and (2) whether the false positive search results obtained by application of all 25 search terms proposed by Plaintiffs could be eliminated by applying to the initial search result set further limiting terms such as "manifold intake valve" or some other limiting term or terms. The court's tentative ruling with respect to the other document requests and interrogatories is as follows: Granted in Part. NNA shall provide Plaintiffs with verified statements of compliance with Document Request Nos. 1, 2, 4, 7, 8, 10, 13, 15, 16, 18, 20, and 21, and verified supplemental responses to Document Requests 6 and 9 affirming its representation that the Consumer Affairs, Salesforce.com, and Techline databases contains records of responsive communications with customers and dealers. Request Nos. 11 and 12 seek relevant information, at least in the absence of a stipulation by NNA that none of its publicly-disseminated information, advertising, and information made available on its website disclosed the alleged defects complained of in Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint. NNA shall produce, in response to Request No. 17, organizational charts and directories only. NNA shall provide further responses to Interrogatories 5 and 7 limited to identification of the two individuals who report to Robert Hankin and the 32 individuals who report to Randy Lieske. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA RAMIREZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO NETWORK MINISTRIES HOUSING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT VINCENT LEA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Lea's Motion for Summary Judgment as to the first and second causes of action in Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant produces sufficient evidence to meet his burden that he did not have any control over the trench in the sidewalk at 2380 Folsom and that he only installed two utility junction boxes above the sidewalk on or about February 8, 2008 and June 25, 2008. (See Declaration of Lea in Support of Motion of Summary Judgment, 1:27-2:8; Exhs. 1-5.). Furthermore, Defendant states that he never removed concrete, or excavated, dug, or worked on the pavement located in the vicinity of 2380 Folsom, nor did he place a board or platform on the sidewalk. (Id. 3:1-8.) The burden shifted to Plaintiff, who has not shown that there is a triable issue as to any material fact. Defendant Lea's purported inconsistent statement regarding the date he installed the second box does not create a triable issue as to whether Defendant Lea installed the platform or ever broke the pavement by digging or excavating at any time, let alone on or about February 8 and June 25, 2008. Plaintiff does not dispute that that Defendant does not own, control, manage, or maintain 2380 Folsom, or the sidewalks adjacent to or near it. (See Plaintiff's Opposition to Lea's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 23.) The court declines plaintiff's request to exercise discretion to deny summary judgment pursuant to CCP 437c(e). Defendant Lea evidence is detailed and he has been subject to cross-examination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MARIA RAMIREZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO NETWORK MINISTRIES HOUSING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT COMCAST OF CALIFORNIA III, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Comcast of California III, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiff's causes of action for premises liability and negligence in her First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is denied. Defendant has failed to shift the burden on either cause of action. Defendant's witness Mr. Graziani had no personal responsibility for supervising the project or any personal knowledge of the work performed by Comcast or others on the block where the incident occurred. See Graziani Deposition at pps. 26:18-27:16; 91:10-22. Defendants' evidence is insufficient to establish that one or more elements of the causes of action for premises liability and negligence cannot be established. (See CCP § 437c(p)(2).) Comcast's written objection to evidence does not comply with CRC 3.1354(b) (must "quote or set forth the objectionable statement or material"). Assuming Comcast is objecting to all of paragraph 5, the objection is overruled as to the first two sentences and sustained as to the remaining paragraph. The Court notes that Plaintiff fails to comply with CRC 3.1350(f) ( opposing separate statement must be "complete with the moving party's references to exhibits") and CRC 3.1350(g) regarding binding documentary evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL L. HUYGHUE VS. WILLIAM R. HAMBRECHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Documents Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Documents Under Seal Granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. The Court finds that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored and relates to account names and numbers and financial information designated as confidential by the judgment debtor. No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest of the judgment debtor in protecting this confidential information. A redacted version of the documents will be available in the Court's public files. The moving party is requested to prepare an order adding this language to the proposed Order to File Documents Under Seal submitted with the moving papers and to present it to the Court for signature. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL L. HUYGHUE VS. WILLIAM R. HAMBRECHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default And To Compel Compliance With Levy, Or In The Alternative To Establish Liability; Memorandum Points And Authorities ---- Redacted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default And To Compel Compliance With Levy, Or In The Alternative To Establish Liability; Memorandum Points And Authorities ---- Redacted Denied without prejudice. Pursuant to Defendant's Consent and Request for Service on Counsel, filed on August 31, 2012, service of all pleadings, writs, notices, orders and other papers are to be served on defendant William R. Hambrecht's counsel. (See CCP §684.020(b).) There is no evidence that this request has been revoked by Defendant or that consent has been revoked by his attorneys. (See CCP § 684.020(b)(2), (3).) Plaintiff has not provided evidence that the required levy documents under CCP § 700.101 were served on Defendant's counsel. The evidence shows that they were served on the service agent of W.R. Hambrecht & Co. This is insufficient service under the circumstances. rulings.Case Title: JOESIAH BELL VS. 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Discovery Responses , Production Of Documents And Depositions; Memo Of P&A; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 4: Plaintiff JOESIAH BELL'S Motion to Compel Discovery Responses, Production of Documents and Depositions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED with respect to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories Nos. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, and 18. Plaintiff's motion is DENIED with respect to Special Interrogatories No. 6. Mass Movement's response to Special Interrogatory No. 6 meets the requirements of CCP Section 2030.230. Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED with respect to Requests for Production Nos. 2, 6 and 9. Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED with respect to the Person Most Knowledgeable depositions. Within 15 days of entry of this order Mass Movement shall serve Plaintiff with verified code-complaint further responses to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories Nos. 5, 7, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17 and 18, and to Plaintiff's Requests for Production Nos. 2, 6 and 9. Mass Movement shall, within 30 days of service of notice of entry of this order produce all documents in its possession, custody, or control, responsive to Plaintiff's requests for production Nos. 2, 6 and 9. Mass Movement shall, within 45 days of service of notice of entry of this order, designate and produce for deposition at Tooker & Antz, 350 Sansome Street, Suite 700, San Francisco, CA 94104, those of its officers, directors, managing agents, employees, or agents who are most qualified to testify on its behalf, to the extent of any information known or reasonably available to Mass Movement, about those matters identified in Plaintiff's Notices of Deposition of Defendant Mass Movement, Inc.'s Person Most Knowledgeable I, II, and III. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC) VS. ROBERT M. LUJAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 14, CROSS DEFENDANT TODD SHIELDS, CLARK GAREN, WIRELESS RECEIVABLES ACQUISITION GROUP, PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC) DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. The Court denies PCC's request for judicial notice because it does not cite the precise basis for judicial notice and the attached documents implicate contested factual issues. Mr. Lujan sufficiently alleges the existence of the Cardmember Agreement. The statute of frauds does not apply because there is nothing showing that the contract could not be performed within a year by its terms. Delaware law appears to apply to this action based on paragraph 18 of the contract. PCC fails to show that federal law or tolling applies. Mr. Lujan alleges sufficient facts to hold the individuals and Wireless liable and a violation of B&P 6129. CC 1714.10 does not apply to this case based on the allegations in paragraphs 23-29 in the amended cross complaint. See CC 1714.10(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT Notice Of Motion To Approve And Enter Consent Judgment Hearing required. The motion requests approval and entry of a "consent judgment." A proposed "Order Granting Motion to Approve and Enter Consent Judgment" has been submitted. The parties are requested to be prepared to address the procedural posture of the case. If a proposed "consent judgment" has already been submitted, counsel are requested to bring a courtesy copy of this document to Department 302 Court by 9:15 a.m. on the morning of the hearing, and, if possible, to send it by email as soon as possible to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with this case name and number in the subject line. =302/mjm
